rankings.idea: DiD-Transformer — Distributional Treatment Effects on Career Trajectories (Revised)
rankings.rationale: Revision strengthens theoretical exposition and deepens connection to structural economics. The parallel trends argument in representation space is now more rigorous. Heterogeneity analysis by occupation and race adds policy relevance. Method remains state-of-art for distributional causal inference.

rankings.idea: DiD-Transformer — Distributional Treatment Effects on Career Trajectories (Revised)
rankings.rationale: Revision maintains methodological rigor while improving accessibility to econometrician audience. Clearer exposition of parallel trends in weight space and formal asymptotic argument would elevate the paper further. Data quality remains excellent.

rankings.idea: DiD-Transformer — Distributional Treatment Effects on Career Trajectories (Revised)
rankings.rationale: Revision bridges representation learning and structural economics more effectively. The distributional treatment effects framework is positioned as contribution to both labor economics and methodological frontier. Strong fit for policy-focused journals.

notes: This is a revision paper. Inherited idea from parent apep_0489 with enhanced methodological exposition and deeper policy analysis.
